name: grimoire
description: Turn a customer's raw pricelist, spreadsheet or ERP export into correct, BOM-aware entities in the Rattle CPQ product configurator (rattleapp.de). Ships 16 skills, 8 subagents, 10 slash commands, 6 JSON Schema output contracts, golden I/O examples, and a zero-dependency Rattle MCP server. Encodes the #1 configurator rule (explicit options for ALL variants), the ingestion contract (raw columns → Rattle entities), 11 configuration rules, 4 anti-patterns, 6 structural checks, 7 idempotent ensure_* operations, the variant-BOM definition layer (usage_subclauses DSL, three option_scalings shapes, numbered options, alt_group alternates, ghost depth-transparency, BOM explosion), and the 15-chapter CE-compliant technical-documentation scaffold (DIN EN ISO 20607, IEC/IEEE 82079-1, MRL 2006/42/EG, MVO (EU) 2023/1230, ISO 7010, CLP/GHS).

api_key.title: Rattle API key
api_key.description: Your Rattle API key (rk_live_… / rk_test_…), from Settings → API in rattleapp.de. Stored in your OS keychain, never in settings.json. Needed only for live-tenant reads and writes — every skill and agent works offline without it.

base_url.title: Rattle API base URL
base_url.description: Override only for a self-hosted or staging instance.

tenant.title: Default tenant
tenant.description: Optional. Resolves per-tenant memory under memory/<tenant>/ and the RATTLE_API_KEY_<TENANT> env var.

allow_writes.title: Let the MCP server write to your live tenant
allow_writes.description: Off by default. While off, the Rattle MCP server refuses every non-GET request, so no agent can mutate your live catalogue by accident. Turn it on only when you mean to apply a reviewed configuration — writes to a live CPQ tenant are irreversible. Prefer routing them through the rattle-config-builder agent, which pauses for confirmation.
